mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals rising capacity utilization and aggregate supply expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of observations during periods of rising INDPRO over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate of more than 0.3 percentage points above its trailing 12-month minimum signals labor market deterioration, triggering risk-averse re-pricing and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its 12-month trailing minimum
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 45% of triggered instances, or the signal's rank correlation with returns is not statistically negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in industrial production and rising unemployment defines a joint recessionary signal that predicts negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings and risk premia re-price concurrently.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change negative and UNRATE 3-month change positive
failure_condition: Joint signal instances do not produce negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power over INDPRO alone (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When monetary policy is restrictive, month-over-month declines in retail sales amplify discount-rate risk and predict negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days because demand weakness cannot be offset by easing expectations.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with FEDFUNDS above 2.5%
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns following RSAFS declines are not significantly stronger when FEDFUNDS > 2.5% compared to FEDFUNDS <= 2.5% (t-test p > 0.05), or occur in fewer than 50% of restrictive-regime triggers.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPI inflation acceleration above 0.3% signals upside inflation risk that lifts real discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the average return post-trigger is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A positive trend in housing starts reflects durable residential investment demand and construction multiplier effects that feed into broad corporate revenue growth, generating positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of observations during periods of rising HOUST over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of elevated federal funds rates and decelerating payroll growth captures a monetary-overkill condition where restrictive policy chokes employment, leading to negative 20-day S&P 500 returns as recession probability rises.
failure_condition: Joint signal instances fail to produce negative 20-day returns in > 55% of cases, or PAYEMS alone achieves equal or higher rank correlation with forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.5% annualized signals insufficient aggregate demand relative to potential, triggering de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or positive GDP surprises do not correspond to positive returns (rank IC < 0.03).
